items.title: In just a couple weeks, StrictlyVC San Francisco brings leaders from TDK Ventures, Replit, and more together

content.content:
The first StrictlyVC of the year will be coming to San Francisco before you know it.

There are still a few tickets available to join us and our stacked speaker lineup April 30, so register today!

items.author: TechCrunch Events

items.title: Google quietly launched an AI dictation app that works offline

content.content: Google's new offline-first dictation app uses Gemma AI models to take on the apps like Wispr Flow.

items.author: Ivan Mehta

items.title: Hate jump scares? This app warns you before they happen

content.content: The app uses iPhone Live Activities to alert you when scary scenes are about to happen, while also tracking what to watch and where to stream it.

items.author: Sarah Perez

items.title: Chrome finally adds a better way to deal with too many open tabs

content.content: Google Chrome's latest update brings vertical tabs and a cleaner Reading Mode to help manage cluttered browsing.

items.author: Sarah Perez

items.title: Russian government hackers broke into thousands of home routers to steal passwords

content.content: Fancy Bear, also known as APT28, has taken over thousands of residential home routers to steal passwords and authentication tokens in a wide-ranging espionage operation.

items.author: Lorenzo Franceschi-Bicchierai

items.title: Apple’s foldable iPhone is on track to launch in September, report says

content.content: The news follows a report from Nikkei Asia on Tuesday that raised concerns the company’s foldable iPhone could be delayed due to challenges during the phone’s engineering test phase.

items.author: Aisha Malik

items.title: Anthropic debuts preview of powerful new AI model Mythos in new cybersecurity initiative

content.content: The new model will be used by a small number of high-profile companies to engage in defensive cybersecurity work.

items.author: Lucas Ropek

items.title: Intel signs on to Elon Musk’s Terafab chips project

content.content: Intel will join SpaceX and Tesla in an effort to build a new U.S. semiconductor factory in Texas, although the scope of its contributions are unclear.

items.author: Tim Fernholz

items.title: Firmus, the ‘Southgate’ AI data center builder backed by Nvidia, hits $5.5B valuation

content.content: Nvidia-backed Asia AI data center provider Firmus has now raised $1.35 billion in six months.

items.author: Julie Bort

items.title: Iranian hackers are targeting American critical infrastructure, US agencies warn

content.content: A joint FBI, NSA, and CISA advisory warns that Iranian hackers have "escalated" their tactics in response to the ongoing U.S.-Israel war with Iran.

items.author: Zack Whittaker

items.title: VC Eclipse has a new $1.3B fund to back — and build — ‘physical AI’ startups

content.content: Eclipse will put some of that money towards incubating, or building startups.

items.author: Kirsten Korosec

items.title: I can’t help rooting for tiny open source AI model maker Arcee

content.content: Arcee is a tiny 26-person U.S. startup that built a high-performing, massive, open source LLM. And it's gaining popularity with OpenClaw users.

items.author: Julie Bort

items.title: How nuclear batteries could speed the race to fusion power

content.content: Avalanche Energy is working on a DARPA project to build a new class of materials capable of turning damaging radiation into electricity.

items.author: Tim De Chant

items.title: X is rolling out automatic translation and photo editing powered by Grok

content.content: New automatic translations and photo editing on X are powered by the company's flagship AI model.

items.author: Ivan Mehta

items.title: Atlassian launches visual AI tools and third-party agents in Confluence

content.content: Confluence users can now create visual assets within the software in addition to new third-party agents working with Lovable, Replit, and Gamma.

items.author: Rebecca Szkutak

items.title: Final 3 days to save up to $500 on your TechCrunch Disrupt 2026 pass

content.content: Save up to $500 on your TechCrunch Disrupt 2026 pass until April 10, 11:59 p.m. PT. Secure your spot at the center of the tech ecosystem. Register today.

items.author: TechCrunch Events

items.title: As YouTube grows on TV, it eyes more interactive video across formats

content.content: YouTube's hiring across roles, partnerships, and a Bengaluru hub shows how it is building out its TV strategy.

items.author: Jagmeet Singh

items.title: Databricks co-founder wins prestigious ACM award, says ‘AGI is here already’

content.content: Matei Zaharia has won the top honor from the Association for Computing Machinery. Now he's working on AI for research and says AGI is simply misunderstood.

items.author: Julie Bort

items.title: Developer of VeraCrypt encryption software says Windows users may face boot-up issues after Microsoft locked his account

content.content: The maker of the popular open source file encryption software VeraCrypt said Microsoft locked his online account, which may prevent device owners from booting up their computers.

items.author: Zack Whittaker

items.title: OpenAI releases a new safety blueprint to address the rise in child sexual exploitation

content.content: OpenAI's new Child Safety Blueprint aims to tackle the alarming rise in child sexual exploitation linked to advancements in AI.

items.author: Lauren Forristal

items.title: Hackers steal and leak sensitive LAPD police documents

content.content: The LAPD said the breach affected “a digital storage system” belonging to the city’s Attorney's Office. The World Leaks extortion gang was reported to be behind the attack.

items.author: Lorenzo Franceschi-Bicchierai

items.title: Astropad’s Workbench reimagines remote desktop for AI agents, not IT support

content.content: Astropad’s Workbench lets users remotely monitor and control AI agents on Mac Minis from iPhone or iPad, with low-latency streaming and mobile access.

items.author: Sarah Perez

items.title: British cryptographer Adam Back denies NYT report that he is Bitcoin creator Satoshi Nakamoto

content.content: Back, a long-time researcher and pioneer in cryptography, denies the allegation that he is the secretive creator of Bitcoin.

items.author: Amanda Silberling

items.title: These are the countries moving to ban social media for children

content.content: Australia was the first country to issue a ban in late 2025, aiming to reduce the pressures and risks that young users may face on social media, including cyberbullying, social media addiction, and exposure to predators.

items.author: Aisha Malik

items.title: Volkswagen begins testing its self-driving microbuses in Los Angeles ahead of launch with Uber

content.content: Volkswagen subsidiary MOIA America still has a long and winding regulatory road before it can launch a commercial robotaxi service.

items.author: Kirsten Korosec

items.title: Tubi is the first streamer to launch a native app within ChatGPT

content.content: Tubi becomes the first streaming service to offer an app integration within ChatGPT, the AI chatbot that millions of users turn to for answers.

items.author: Lauren Forristal

items.title: Hack-for-hire group caught targeting Android devices and iCloud backups

content.content: Security researchers exposed a spying campaign by a hack-for-hire group that used Android spyware and phishing to steal iCloud credentials and hack victims’ devices.

items.author: Lorenzo Franceschi-Bicchierai

items.title: Meta debuts the Muse Spark model in a ‘ground-up overhaul’ of its AI

content.content: This is the first model Meta has released under its Superintelligence Labs, led by former Scale AI CEO Alexandr Wang.

items.author: Amanda Silberling

items.title: Amazon to end support for older Kindle devices

content.content: Amazon told Kindle owners this week that it's ending support for all e-readers released in 2012 or earlier, making them virtually unable to load any new content.

items.author: Lauren Forristal

items.title: AWS boss explains why investing billions in both Anthropic and OpenAI is an OK conflict

content.content: AWS has an ingrained culture of handling competition, he explained, because the cloud giant also competes with its partners.

items.author: Julie Bort

items.title: A self-driving car in Texas hit and killed a mother duck, sparking neighborhood outrage

content.content: An Avride autonomous vehicle near Austin hit the duck, facing backlash from the community. "It didn't slow down or hesitate at all, just steamrolled right through," according to a witness.

items.author: Kirsten Korosec

items.title: Canva doubles down on AI and marketing automation with Simtheory, Ortto acquisitions

content.content: Canva says the acquisitions add strengths in agentic AI, data infrastructure, marketing automation, and customer engagement.

items.author: Aisha Malik

items.title: Poke makes using AI agents as easy as sending a text

content.content: Poke brings AI agents to everyday users via text message by handling tasks and automations without complex setup, apps, or technical know-how.

items.author: Sarah Perez

items.title: WireGuard VPN developer can’t ship software updates after Microsoft locks account

content.content: The popular open source VPN maker is the second high-profile developer to say Microsoft locked his account without notifying him and is blocking their ability to send software updates to users.

items.author: Zack Whittaker

items.title: Ex-Tesla engineer’s startup taps Pronto to help automate a copper mine

content.content: Pronto's autonomous haulage trucks are about to start operating at Mariana Minerals' Utah copper mine -- the first such deal since Pronto got acquired by Travis Kalanick's Atoms Inc.

items.author: Sean O'Kane

items.title: Waymo robotaxis are tracking potholes and sharing that data with Waze users

content.content: Waymo and Waze announced a data-sharing pilot program that will funnel pothole data collected by robotaxis to a free Waze platform designed for cities.

items.author: Kirsten Korosec

items.title: Radify’s sci-fi plasma reactors could break China’s dominance of rare-earth elements

content.content: Radify Metals is developing a new way to process a variety of metals that promises to be pollution free.

items.author: Tim De Chant

items.title: Collide Capital raises $95M fund to back fintech, future-of-work startups

content.content: Collide Capital, founded by Brian Hollins and Aaron Samuels, announced the close of a $95 million Fund II.

items.author: Dominic-Madori Davis

items.title: This founder helped build SpaceX’s most powerful rocket engine. Now he’s building a ‘fighter jet for orbit.’

content.content: The company's novel rocket engine could be a game changer for the U.S. military.

items.author: Tim Fernholz

items.title: Instagram expands its movie-inspired content restrictions for teens internationally

content.content: Instagram debuted its movie-rating-inspired content settings in limited countries in 2025.

items.author: Ivan Mehta

items.title: Spotify now lets everyone turn off videos in its app

content.content: The controls can be used to choose an audio-first experience on the app or a video-enhanced one, Spotify says.

items.author: Aisha Malik

items.title: Final 2 days to save up to $500 on your TechCrunch Disrupt 2026 ticket

content.content: Ticket discounts of up to $500 will end tomorrow, April 10, at 11:59 p.m. PT. After that, prices for TechCrunch Disrupt 2026 go up again. Miss this, and you’ll be paying more for the same access to one of the most anticipated tech events of the year. Register now to lock in these savings.

items.author: TechCrunch Events

items.title: Amazon CEO takes aim at Nvidia, Intel, Starlink, more in annual shareholder letter

content.content: Andy Jassy's annual shareholder letter reads something like a diss track to a wide range of competitors as he defends spending $200 billion in capex.

items.author: Julie Bort

items.title: Hacker stole £700,000 from UK energy company by redirecting payment

content.content: The U.K. energy company said a redirected payment meant for a contractor instead landed in a hacker's bank account.

items.author: Zack Whittaker

items.title: X brings back Voice Notes to X Chat

content.content: X Chat brings back Voice Notes to DMs and group chats after removing the option during the upgrade.

items.author: Sarah Perez

items.title: Avec’s Tinder-style email app allows you to swipe through your inbox

content.content: Users can use the in-built voice transcription tool to reply to emails

items.author: Ivan Mehta

items.title: Sierra’s Bret Taylor says the era of clicking buttons is over

content.content: Last month, Sierra launched Ghostwriter, an agent designed to build other agents. With this “agent as a service” tool, the startup intends to replace traditional click-based web applications with natural language. Users simply describe what they need, prompting Ghostwriter to autonomously create and deploy a specialized agent to execute the task.

items.author: Marina Temkin

items.title: You can now edit your Instagram comments

content.content: Instagram users can now edit comments, which is a long-awaited fix for anyone who’s ever had to delete and re-post a comment just to fix a typo.

items.author: Lauren Forristal

items.title: How to make the Startup Battlefield Top 20 — and what every company gets regardless

content.content: Every founder who applies to Startup Battlefield wants the same thing: the Disrupt Main Stage. Here’s how to get there and why the opportunity starts well before the main stage.

items.author: Isabelle Johannessen

items.title: Google and Intel deepen AI infrastructure partnership

content.content: The two tech giants are looking to co-develop custom chips, at a time when demand for CPUs is high due to a growing global shortage.

items.author: Rebecca Szkutak
